blogs.title: International Day Of Families
blogs.description:
A day which provides an opportunity to promote awareness of issues relating to families and to increase the knowledge of the social, economic, and 
demographic processes affecting families.

blogs.title: International Day Against Homophobia,Biphobia and  Transphobia.
blogs.description:
What is IDAHOBIT?
The International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia, and Biphobia was created in 2004 to draw attention to the violence and discrimination experienced by lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, intersex people, and all of those with diverse sexual orientations, gender identities or expressions, and sex characteristics.

Initially managed by the IDAHO Committee, the initiative is now collectively managed in collaboration between regional and thematic networks working to advance the rights of people with diverse sexual orientations, gender identities or expressions, and sex characteristics. This collaboration brings together organisations and initiatives at global, regional, national, and local levels.

The International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia, and Biphobia is currently celebrated in more than 130 countries, including 37 where same-sex acts are illegal. Thousands of initiatives, big and small, are reported throughout the planet.

The International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia, and Biphobia has received official recognition from several States, international institutions such as the European Parliament, and by countless local authorities. Most United Nations agencies also mark the Day with specific events.

blogs.title: World day for cultural diversity for dialog and development
National Accessability Week
blogs.description: A week to celebrate the valuable contributions and accomplishments of Canadians with disabilities and to recognize the need to promote a culture of  inclusion to combat the ongoing discrimination against persons with disabilities.

blogs.title: Asian Heritage Month
blogs.description: Asian Heritage Month is an opportunity for us to learn more about the many achievements and contributions of Canadians of Asian heritage who, throughout our history, have done so much to make Canada the country we know and love.

blogs.title: Jewish Heritage Month
blogs.description: Canada is home to an estimated 400,000 people of Jewish heritage, making it the fourth-largest Jewish community in the world. During this month, we also recognize the courage, determination, and resilience that members of Jewish communities have shown throughout history.This month, celebrate the history and culture of Jewish Canadians. Learning about their diverse heritage and religion allows us to better understand those around us. This month, commit to learning about how to fight antisemitism.

blogs.title: Mental Health Awareness
blogs.description: Mental health is wealth, especially during Mental Health Awareness Month, which is celebrated in May. The stigma around mental health and treatment has long existed, even though this has started to change. Aims to raise awareness and educate the public about mental illnesses and reduce the stigma that surrounds mental illnesses.

blogs.title: MS Awareness Month
blogs.description:
World MS Day is an annual event aimed at raising public understanding and support for those living with Multiple Sclerosis (MS), a chronic condition affecting the central nervous system. This day highlights the challenges faced by people with MS, promoting advocacy for improved resources,
treatment, and research. Observed internationally, the day includes educational events, fundraising, and support gatherings, symbolized by orange for hope and resilience.
